questions.q: If the constant term in the expansion of $\left(1+2 x-3 x^3\right)\left(\frac{3}{2} x^2-\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^9$ is $\mathrm{p}$, then $108 \mathrm{p}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \left(1+2 x-3 x^3\right)\left(\frac{3}{2} x^2-\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^9 \\
& \text { General term } m\left(\frac{3}{2} x^2-\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^9 \\
& ={ }^9 C_r \cdot \frac{3^{9-2 r}}{2^{9-r}}(-1)^r \cdot x^{18-3 r}
\end{aligned}$
Put $r=6$ to get coeff. of $x^0={ }^9 C_6 \cdot \frac{1}{6^3} \cdot x^0=\frac{7}{18} x^0$
Put $\mathrm{r}=7$ to get coeff. of $\mathrm{x}^{-3}={ }^9 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}} \cdot \frac{3^{-5}}{2^2}(-1)^7 \cdot \mathrm{x}^{-3}$
$\begin{aligned}
& =-{ }^9 C_7 \cdot \frac{1}{3^5 \cdot 2^2} \cdot x^{-3}=\frac{-1}{27} x^{-3} \\
& \left(1+2 x-3 x^3\right)\left(\frac{7}{18} x^0-\frac{1}{27} x^{-3}\right) \\
& \frac{7}{18}+\frac{3}{27}=\frac{7}{18}+\frac{1}{9}=\frac{7+2}{18}=\frac{9}{18}=\frac{1}{2} \\
& \therefore 108 \cdot \frac{1}{2}=54
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.solution:

questions.solution:
We know that,

General term in expansion of (7)1/2+(11)1/6824 is given by,

tr+1=Cr824·7824-r2·11r6

For integral term, r must be multiple of 6 and should be less than 824

So, the series will be r=0,6,12, ..... 822

Now, finding number of terms we get,

Hence, total integral terms will be 138

Given,

=1+x21-x1+x3x35

=1+x171-xx15

Now for coefficient of x3, we will find coefficient of x18 in expansion of 1+x17 which is not possible and x17 in expansion of -1+x17 which will be -C1717=-1

And for coefficient of x-13 we will coefficient of x2 in expansion of 1+x17 which will be C217 and coefficient of x in expansion of -1+x17 which will be -C117, so the coefficient of x-13 will be C217-C117=136-17=119

questions.q: Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the sum and the product of all the non-zero solutions of the equation $(\bar{z})^2+|z|=0$, $z \in$ C. Then $4\left(\alpha^2+\beta^2\right)$ is equal to :
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{ll}z=x+i y & \\ \bar{z}=x-i y & \\ \bar{z}^2=x^2-y^2-2 i x y & \\ \Rightarrow x^2-y^2-2 i x y+\sqrt{x^2+y^2}=0 \\ \Rightarrow x=0 \quad \text { or } & y=0 \\ -y^2+|y|=0 & x^2+|x|=0 \\ |y|=|y|^2 & \Rightarrow x=0 \\ y=0, \pm 1 & \Rightarrow \alpha=i-i=0 \\ \Rightarrow i,-i & \beta=i(-i)=1 \\ \text { are roots } & 4(0+1)=4\end{array}$

$\begin{aligned} & \text { Required area } \\ & =\text { Area of semi-circle }- \text { area of sector A } \\ & \frac{1}{2} \pi(2)^2-\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & =\frac{3 \pi}{2}\end{aligned}$

If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="|" open="|"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mi>z</mi></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are integers, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></math> equals __________
questions.solution:
Given: z+2-3i≤1

Putting, z=x+iy.

⇒x+22+y-32≤1   ...i

For the circle represented in equation i, centre is -2,3 and radius, r=1.

It is given that, z1+i+z1-i≤-8

⇒x+iy1+i+x-iy1-i≤-8

⇒x+ix-y+iy+x-ix-iy-y≤-8

⇒2x-2y≤-8

⇒x-y+4=0

So, the line passing through 3,-2 and perpendicular to x-y+4=0 is given by, x+y-1=0.

So, the distance of line x-y+4=0 from the centre -2,3 is given by,

d=-2-3+42

⇒d=12

Now, x+y-1=0 can be rewritten as,

x+2-12=y-312= CA or CB

For CA=-12 and CB=1, A≡-32,52 and B≡-12-2, 12+3.

⇒z12=2+122+3+122

⇒z12=4+12+22+9+12+32

⇒z12=14+52

⇒z22=294+254

⇒z22=17

⇒z12+2z22=31+52

⇒α=31, β=5

⇒α+β=36

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> denotes the number of solutions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>−</mo><mi>i</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mfrac><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mi>z</mi></mfenced><mrow><mi>arg</mi><mfenced><mi>z</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>4</mn></msup><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>−</mo><msqrt><mi>π</mi></msqrt><mo>·</mo><mi>i</mi></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mi>π</mi></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><msqrt><mi>π</mi></msqrt><mo>−</mo><mi>i</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mi>π</mi></msqrt><mo>·</mo><mi>i</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></math>, then the distance of the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> from the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>7</mn></math> is ______
questions.solution:
Given,

1−ix=2x

⇒2x=2x

⇒x=0

Hence, the number of solutions will be one, so α=1

Now, solving

z=π41+i41−π·iπ+i+π−i1+π·i

⇒z=π41+i4π−πi−i−ππ+1+π−i−πi−π1+π

⇒z=π41+i4−2πi−2iπ+1

⇒z=πi21+i4

⇒z=−πi21+4i+6i2+4i3+1

⇒z=2πi

So, argz=π2

⇒β=zargz=2ππ2=4

Hence, the distance from α,β≡1, 4 to 4x−3y=7 will be 4-3×4-742+32=155=3

Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mrow><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>−</mo><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> equals:
questions.solution:
Given,

z−1=1

Now, let z=x+iy

⇒x−12+y2=1   ...i

And 

2−1z+z¯-iz-z¯=22

⇒2−12x−i2iy=22

⇒2−1x+y=2   ...ii

Solving i and ii,

⇒x-12+2-2-1x2=1

⇒x2+1-2x+2+2-12x2-222-1x=1

⇒x2+1-2x+2+2+1-22x2-4x+22x=1

⇒x2-2x+2+3x2-2x22-4x+22x=0

⇒4x2-2x22-6x+22x+2=0

⇒4-22x2+-6+22x+2=0

⇒4-22x2+-4+22x-2x+2=0

⇒4-22xx-1-2x-1=0

⇒x-14-22x-2=0

Either x=1 or x=12−2   ...iii

On solving ii and iii we get

For x=1⇒y=1⇒z2=1+i and for x=12−2⇒y=2−12⇒z1=1+12+i2

⇒2z1−z22=12+12+i−1+i2

⇒2z1−z22=1+2+i−1+i2

⇒2z1−z22=22

⇒2z1−z22=2

questions.q: Consider the matrices : $A=\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & -5 \\ 3 & m\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l}20 \\ m\end{array}\right]$ and $X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]$. Let the set of all $m$, for which the system of equations $A X=B$ has a negative solution (i.e., $x < 0$ and $y < 0$ ), be the interval $(a, b)$. Then $8 \int_a^b|A| d m$ is equal to_________
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & A=\left(\begin{array}{cc}2 & -5 \\ 3 & m\end{array}\right), B=\left(\begin{array}{c}20 \\ m\end{array}\right) \\ & X=\left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right) \\ & 2 x-5 y=20 \ldots(1)\\ & 3 x+m y=m \ldots(2)\\ & \Rightarrow y=\frac{2 m-60}{2 m+15} \\ & y < 0 \Rightarrow m \in\left(\frac{-15}{2}, 30\right)\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{x}=\frac{25 \mathrm{~m}}{2 \mathrm{~m}+15} \\ & \mathrm{x} < 0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{m} \in\left(\frac{-15}{2}, 0\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{m} \in\left(\frac{-15}{2}, 0\right) \\ & |\mathrm{A}|=2 \mathrm{~m}+15 \end{aligned}$
Now, $\begin{aligned} & 8 \int_{\frac{-15}{2}}^0(2 \mathrm{~m}+15) \mathrm{dm}=8\left\{\mathrm{~m}^2+15 \mathrm{~m}\right\}_{\frac{-15}{2}}^0 \\ & \Rightarrow 8\left\{-\left(\frac{225}{4}-\frac{225}{2}\right)\right\} \\ & =8 \times \frac{225}{4}=450 \end{aligned}$

a,b,c are in A.P.

Now, on comparing above equation with ax+by+c=0 we get,

 The line ax+by+c=0 passes through fixed point 1,-2

∴P=(1,-2)

Now, we know that for infinite solution,

D=11125α123=0

And D1=611β5α423=0⇒β=6

So, the point Q=α,β=(8,6)

questions.q: Let $\alpha \in(0, \infty)$ and $A=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 2 & \alpha \\ 1 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]$. If $\operatorname{det}\left(\operatorname{adj}\left(2 A-A^T\right) \cdot \operatorname{adj}\left(A-2 A^T\right)\right)=2^8$, then $(\operatorname{det}(A))^2$ is equal to:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \left|\operatorname{adj}\left(\mathrm{A}-2 \mathrm{~A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right)\left(2 \mathrm{~A}-\mathrm{A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right)\right|=28 \\ & \left|\left(\mathrm{~A}-2 \mathrm{~A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right)\left(2 \mathrm{~A}-\mathrm{A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right)\right|=24 \\ & \left|\mathrm{~A}-2 \mathrm{~A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right|\left|2 \mathrm{~A}-\mathrm{A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right|= \pm 16 \\ & \left(\mathrm{~A}-2 \mathrm{~A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right)^{\mathrm{T}}=\mathrm{A}^{\mathrm{T}}-2 \mathrm{~A} \\ & \left|\mathrm{~A}-2 \mathrm{~A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right|=\left|\mathrm{A}^{\mathrm{T}}-2 \mathrm{~A}\right| \\ & \Rightarrow\left|\mathrm{A}-2 \mathrm{~A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right|^2=16 \\ & \left|\mathrm{~A}-2 \mathrm{~A}^{\mathrm{T}}\right|= \pm 4 \\ & {\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & \alpha \\ 1 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & 2 & 0 \\ 4 & 0 & 2 \\ 2 \alpha & 2 & 4\end{array}\right]} \\ & \left|\begin{array}{lll}-1 & 0 & \alpha \\ -3 & 0 & -1 \\ -2 \alpha & -1 & -2\end{array}\right| \\ & 1+3 \alpha=4 \\ & 3 \alpha=3 \\ & \alpha=1 \\ & |\mathrm{~A}|=\left|\begin{array}{lll}1 & 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right|=-1-3=-4 \\ & |\mathrm{~A}|^2=16\end{aligned}$

questions.q: Let $A$ and $B$ be two square matrices of order 3 such that $|A|=3$ and $|B|=2$. Then $\left|\mathrm{A}^{\mathrm{T}} \mathrm{A}(\operatorname{adj}(2 \mathrm{~A}))^{-1}(\operatorname{adj}(4 \mathrm{~B}))(\operatorname{adj}(\mathrm{AB}))^{-1} \mathrm{AA}^{\mathrm{T}}\right|$ is equal to :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & |\mathrm{A}|=3,|\mathrm{~B}|=2 \\ & \left|\mathrm{~A}^{\mathrm{T}} \mathrm{A}(\operatorname{adj}(2 \mathrm{~A}))^{-1}(\operatorname{adj}(4 \mathrm{~B}))(\operatorname{adj}(\mathrm{AB}))^{-1} \mathrm{AA}^{\mathrm{T}}\right|\end{aligned}$ \(\begin{array}{ccc} =3 \times 3 \times \mid \left(\operatorname{adj}(2 \mathrm{~A})^{-1}|\times| \operatorname{adj}(4 \mathrm{~B})| \times|(\operatorname{adj}(\mathrm{AB}))^{-1}\mid \times 3 \times 3\right. \\ \downarrow \qquad\qquad \downarrow \qquad\qquad \downarrow \\ \frac{1}{|\operatorname{adj}(2 \mathrm{~A})|} \qquad 2^{12} \times 2^2 \qquad \frac{1}{|\operatorname{adj}(\mathrm{AB})|} \end{array}\)
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2^6|\operatorname{adj} \mathrm{A}|} \quad=\frac{1}{|\operatorname{adj} \mathrm{B} \cdot \operatorname{adj} \mathrm{A}|} \\ & =\frac{1}{2^6 \cdot 3^2} \quad=\frac{1}{2^2 \cdot 3^2} \\ & =3^4 \cdot \frac{1}{2^6 \cdot 3^2} \cdot 2^{12} \cdot 2^2 \cdot \frac{1}{2^2 \cdot 3^2}=64 \\ & \end{aligned}$

questions.q: If $A$ is a square matrix of order 3 such that $\operatorname{det}(A)=3$ and $\operatorname{det}\left(\operatorname{adj}\left(-4 \operatorname{adj}\left(-3 \operatorname{adj}\left(3 \operatorname{adj}\left((2 \mathrm{~A})^{-1}\right)\right)\right)\right)\right)=2^{\mathrm{m}} 3^{\mathrm{n}}$, then $\mathrm{m}+2 \mathrm{n}$ is equal to :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & |\mathrm{A}|=3 \\ & \mid \operatorname{adj}\left(-4 \operatorname{adj}\left(-3 \operatorname{adj}\left(3 \operatorname{adj}\left((2 \mathrm{~A})^{-1}\right)\right)\right) \mid\right. \\ & \mid-4 \operatorname{adj}\left(-\left.3 \operatorname{adj}\left(3 \operatorname{adj}(2 \mathrm{~A})^{-1}\right)\right|^2\right. \\ & 4^6\left|\operatorname{adj}\left(-3 \operatorname{adj}\left(3 \operatorname{adj}(2 \mathrm{~A})^{-1}\right)\right)\right|^2 \\ & 2^{12} \cdot 3^{12}\left|3 \operatorname{adj}(2 \mathrm{~A})^{-1}\right|^8 \\ & 2^{12} \cdot 3^{12} \cdot 3^{24}\left|\operatorname{adj}(2 \mathrm{~A})^{-1}\right|^8\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & 2^{12} \cdot 3^{36}\left|(2 \mathrm{~A})^{-1}\right|^{16} \\ & 2^{12} \cdot 3^{36} \frac{1}{|2 \mathrm{~A}|^{16}} \\ & 2^{12} \cdot 3^{36} \frac{1}{2^{48}|\mathrm{~A}|^{16}} \\ & 2^{12} \cdot 3^{36} \frac{1}{2^{48} \cdot 3^{16}} \\ & \frac{3^{20}}{2^{36}}=2^{-36} \cdot 3^{20} \\ & \mathrm{~m}=-36 \quad \mathrm{n}=20 \\ & \mathrm{~m}+2 \mathrm{n}=4\end{aligned}$

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> be a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><mn>2</mn></math> real matrix and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> be the identity matrix of order <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>|</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>xI</mi><mo>|</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the sum of the diagonal elements of the matrix <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is _____.
questions.solution:
Given: |A-xI|=0 have roots -1 and 3.

Let, A=abcd

⇒A-xI=abcd-x00x

⇒A-xI=a-xbcd-x

⇒A-xI=a-xd-x-bc

⇒A-xI=ad-ax-dx+x2-bc

⇒A-xI=x2-a+dx+ad-bc

Sum of roots, a+d=2

Product of roots, ad-bc=-3

Now finding matrix A2,

⇒A2=abcd×abcd

⇒A2=a2+bcab+bdac+cdbc+d2

Sum of diagonal elements, S=a2+bc+bc+d2

⇒S=a2+2bc+d2

⇒S=(a+d)2-2ad-bc

⇒S=(2)2-2-3

⇒S=4+6

⇒S=10
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>I</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>−</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>M</mi><msup><mi>M</mi><mi>T</mi></msup><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> is real matrix of order <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn></math> such that the relation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>M</mi><mi>T</mi></msup><mi>M</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>I</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> holds. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi></math> is a real number such that the relation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>X</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>λ</mi><mi>X</mi></math> holds for some non-zero real matrix <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi></math> of order <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the sum of squares of all possible values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi></math> is equal to:
questions.solution:
Given: A=I2−2MMT

⇒A2=I2−2MMTI2−2MMT

⇒A2=I2−2MMT−2MMT+4MMTMMT

⇒A2=I2−4MMT+4MMT as MTM=I1

⇒A2=I2

Also, AX=λX

⇒A2X=λAX

⇒X=λλX as A2=I2 & AX=λX

⇒X=λ2X

⇒Xλ2−1=0

⇒λ2=1

⇒λ=±1

So, sum of square of all possible values =2

Let R=x000y000z be a non-zero 3×3 matrix, where xsinθ=ysinθ+2π3=zsinθ+4π3 ≠0,θ∈(0,2π). For a square matrix M, let TraceM denote the sum of all the diagonal entries of M. Then, among the statements:
I Trace(R)=0
(II) If Trace(adj(adj(R))=0, then R has exactly one non-zero entry.
questions.solution:
Given,

xsinθ=ysinθ+2π3=zsinθ+4π3≠0, 

⇒y=xsinθsinθ+2π3, z=xsinθsinθ+4π3

Now, finding the trace of matrix we get,

x+y+z=x+xsinθsinθ+2π3+xsinθsinθ+4π3

⇒x+y+z=xsinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3+xsinθsinθ+4π3+xsinθsinθ+2π3sinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3

⇒x+y+z=xsinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3+xsinθsinθ+4π3+sinθ+2π3sinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3

⇒x+y+z=x2·2sinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3+xsinθ2sinθ+πcosπ3sinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3

⇒x+y+z=x2·cos2π3-cos2θ+2π+xsinθ-2sinθ·12sinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3

⇒x+y+z=x2·-12-cos2θ-xsin2θsinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3

⇒x+y+z=-x-2xcos2θ-4xsin2θ4sinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3

⇒x+y+z=-x-2x1-2sin2θ-4xsin2θ4sinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3

⇒x+y+z=-3x+4xsin2θ-4xsin2θ4sinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3

⇒x+y+z=-3x4sinθ+2π3sinθ+4π3≠0

I Trace (R)=x+y+z≠0

 ⇒ Statement (i) is False

Now, finding Adj(Adj(R))

Now, using the property Adj(Adj(R))=R|R| we get,

Trace (Adj(Adj(R)))=xyz(x+y+z)≠0

{As x+y+z≠0 proved above and x,y & z are non zero}

Hence, Trace(adj(adj(R))=0 is a false statement,

So, neither I nor II are true

questions.q: The number of ways of getting a sum 16 on throwing a dice four times is______
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \left(x^1+x^2 \ldots+x^6\right)^4 \\ & x^4 \cdot\left(\frac{1-x^6}{1-x}\right)^4 \\ & x^4 \cdot\left(1-x^6\right)^4 \cdot(1-x)^{-4} \\ & x^4\left[1-4 x^6+6 x^{12} \ldots .\right]\left[(1-x)^{-4}\right] \\ & \left(x^4-4 x^{10}+6 x^{16} \ldots\right)(1-x)^{-4} \\ & \left(x^4-4 x^{10}+6 x^{16}\right)\left(1+{ }^{15} C_{12} x^{12}+{ }^9 C_6 x^6 \ldots .\right) \\ & \left({ }^{15} C_{12}-4 \cdot{ }^9 C_6+6\right) x^{16} \\ & \left({ }^{15} C_3-4 \cdot{ }^9 C_6+6\right) \\ & =35 \times 13-6 \times 8 \times 7+6 \\ & =455-336+6 \\ & =125\end{aligned}$

questions.q: The number of integers, between 100 and 1000 having the sum of their digits equals to 14 , is _________
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{N}=\mathrm{ab} \mathrm{c}$ (i)
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { All distinct digits } \\
& \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c}=14 \\
& \mathrm{a} \geq 1 \\
& \mathrm{~b}, \mathrm{c} \in\{0 \text { to } 9\}
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{lll} 
by hit \& trial : & 8 cases \\
(6,5,3) & (8,6,0) & (9,4,1) \\
(7,6,1) & (8,5,1) & (9,3,2) \\
(7,5,2) & (8,4,2) & \\
(7,4,3) & (9,5,0) &
\end{array}$
(ii) 2 same, 1 diff $\quad a=b$;c
$2 \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{c}=14$
by values :
$\begin{aligned}
& \left.\begin{array}{l}
(3,8) \\
(4,6) \\
(5,4) \\
(6,2) \\
(7,0)
\end{array}\right\} \frac{3 !}{2 !} \times 5-1 \\
& =14 \text { cases }
\end{aligned}$
(iii) all same :
$\begin{aligned}
& \begin{array}{l}
3 a=14 \\
a=\frac{14}{3} \times \text { rejected } \\
0 \text { cases }
\end{array}
\end{aligned}$
Hence, Total cases:
$\begin{aligned}
& 8 \times 3 !+2 \times(4)+14 \\
& =48+22 \\
& =70
\end{aligned}$

questions.solution:
Let us initially distribute 2 apples to each children.

So, the remaining 15 apples are to be distributed such that each children can get any number of apples.

So, the required number of ways will be given by N=Cr-1n+r-1.

⇒N=C217

Given:

Out of 20 numbered lines, even numbered lines are concurrent, odd number lines are parallel.

So, the maximum number of intersection points will be given by,

N=1→point of intersection when both lines are even+0→point of intersection when both lines are parallel+C110×C110→point of intersection when 1 odd and 1 even line meets

⇒N=1+0+100

⇒N=101

questions.q: In an examination of Mathematics paper, there are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn></math> questions of equal marks and the question paper is divided into three sections : <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. A student is required to attempt total <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> questions taking at least <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> questions from each section. If section <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> questions, section <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> questions and section <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> questions, then the total number of ways a student can select <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> questions is _________.
questions.solution:
Given,

Mathematics paper has 20 questions of equal marks

and have three sections A, B & C, also A has 8 questions, B has 6 questions and C has 6 questions,

Now, if 4 questions from each section are selected,

then remaining 3 questions can be selected either in (1, 1,1) or (3,0,0) or (2,1,0) {as we have already selected 4×3=12 questions}

So, the total ways will be=C58·C56·C56+C68C56·C46×2+C58·C66·C46×2+C48·C66·C56×2+C78·C46·C46

=56·6·6+28·6·15·2+56·15·2+70·6·2+8·15·15

=2016+5040+1680+840+1800=11376

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is the number of ways five different employees can sit into four indistinguishable offices where any office may have any number of persons including zero, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to:
questions.solution:
Given,

Rooms are identical,

So, total ways to distribute 5 employees into 4 rooms are:

5,0,0,0⇒1 way

4,1,0,0⇒5!4!=5 ways

3,2,0,0⇒5!3!2!=10 ways

2,2,0,1⇒5!2!2!2!=15 ways

2,1,1,1⇒5!2!1!33!=10 ways

3,1,1,0⇒5!3!2!=10 ways

Total ⇒1+5+10+15+10+10=51 ways

questions.q: Number of ways of arranging <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> identical books into <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> identical shelves where any number of shelves may remain empty is equal to
questions.solution:
We have 4 identical shelves in which any number of shelves can be empty.

So, we will put 3, 2, 1, 0 shelves empty one by one.

Also, the non-empty shelves won't require internal arrangement as all the shelves are identical.  

3 Shelf empty: 8,0,0,0→1 way

2 shelf empty: (7,1,0,0)(6,2,0,0)(5,3,0,0)(4,4,0,0)→4 ways

1 shelf empty: (6,1,1,0)(3,3,2,0)(5,2,1,0)(4,2,2,0)(4,3,1,0)→5ways

0 shelf empty: (1,2,3,2)(5,1,1,1)(2,2,2,2)(3,3,1,1)(4,2,1,1)→5 ways

Total =15 ways

questions.q: In a tournament, a team plays 10 matches with probabilities of winning and losing each match as $\frac{1}{3}$ and $\frac{2}{3}$ respectively. Let $x$ be the number of matches that the team wins, and $y$ be the number of matches that team loses. If the probability $\mathrm{P}(|x-y| \leq$ 2) is $p$, then $3^9 p$ equals ______
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & P(W)=\frac{1}{3} \quad P(L)=\frac{2}{3} \\ & x=\text { number of matches that team wins } \\ & y=\text { number of matches that team loses } \\ & |x-y| \leq 2 \text { and } x+y=10 \\ & |x-y|=0,1,2 \quad x, y \in N\end{aligned}$
Case-I
$\begin{aligned}
& -I:|x-y|=0 \Rightarrow x=y \\
& \because x+y=10 \Rightarrow x=5=y \\
& P(|x-y|=0)={ }^{10} C_5\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^5\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^5
\end{aligned}$
Case-II : $|\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y}|=1 \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y}= \pm 1$
$\begin{array}{|c|c|}
\hline \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{y}+1 & \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{y}-1 \\
\hline\because \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=10 & \because \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=10 \\
\hline 2 \mathrm{y}=9 & 2 \mathrm{y}=11 \\
\hline \text{Not possible} & \text{Not possible} \\
\hline
\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Case-III : }|\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y}|=2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y}= \pm 2 \\ & x-y=2 \quad \text { OR } \quad x-y=-2 \\ & \because \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=10 \quad \because \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=10 \\ & x=6, y=4 \quad x=4, y=6 \\ & \mathrm{P}(|\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y}|=2)={ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_6\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^6\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^4+{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_4\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^4\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^6 \\ & \mathrm{p}={ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_5 \frac{2^5}{3^{10}}+{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_6 \frac{2^4}{3^{10}}+{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_4 \frac{2^6}{3^{10}} \\ & 3^9 \mathrm{p}=\frac{1}{3}\left({ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_5 2^5+{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_6 2^4+{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_4 2^6\right) \\ & =8288 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.q: A company has two plants $A$ and $B$ to manufacture motorcycles. $60 \%$ motorcycles are manufactured at plant $A$ and the remaining are manufactured at plant $B .80 \%$ of the motorcycles manufactured at plant $A$ are rated of the standard quality, while $90 \%$ of the motorcycles manufactured at plant $B$ are rated of the standard quality. A motorcycle picked up randomly from the total production is found to be of the standard quality. If $p$ is the probability that it was manufactured at plant $B$, then $126 p$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{|l|c|c|}
\hline & \text{A} & \text{B} \\
\hline \text{Manufactured} & 60 \% & 40 \% \\
\hline \text{Standard quality} & 80 \% & 90 \% \\
\hline
\end{array}$
$\mathrm{P}$ (Manufactured at $\mathrm{B} /$ found standard quality $)=$ ?
A : Found S.Q
B : Manufacture B
$\mathrm{C}$ : Manufacture A
$\begin{aligned}
& P\left(E_1\right)=\frac{40}{100} \\
& P\left(E_2\right)=\frac{60}{100} \\
& P\left(A / E_1\right)=\frac{90}{100} \\
& P\left(A / E_2\right)=\frac{80}{100} \\
& \because P\left(E_1 / A\right)=\frac{P\left(A / E_1\right) P\left(E_1\right)}{P\left(A / E_1\right) P\left(E_1\right)+P\left(A / E_2\right) P\left(E_2\right)}=\frac{3}{7} \\
& \therefore 126 P=54
\end{aligned}$

questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x+y=24, x, y \in N \\ & A M>G M \Rightarrow x y \leq 144 \\ & x y \geq 108\end{aligned}$ Favorable pairs of $(x, y)$ are $\begin{aligned} & (13,11),(12,12),(14,10),(15,9),(16,8), \\ & (17,7),(18,6),(6,18),(7,17),(8,16),(9,15), \\ & (10,14),(11,13)\end{aligned}$ i.e. 13 cases

questions.q: Let $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}$ and $\mathrm{c}$ denote the outcome of three independent rolls of a fair tetrahedral die, whose four faces are marked $1,2,3,4$. If the probability that $a x^2+b x+c=0$ has all real roots is $\frac{m}{n}$, $\operatorname{gcd}(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})=1$, then $\mathrm{m}+\mathrm{n}$ is equal to ________
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c} \in\{1,2,3,4\}$
Tetrahedral dice $a x^2+b x+c=0$ has all real roots $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{D} \geq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{b}^2-4 \mathrm{ac} \geq 0 \end{aligned}$
Let $b=1 \Rightarrow 1-4 a c \geq 0$ (Not feasible) $\begin{aligned} & b=2 \Rightarrow 4-4 a c \geq 0 \\ & 1 \geq a c \Rightarrow a=1, c=1, \\ & b=3 \Rightarrow 9-4 a c \geq 0 \\ & \frac{9}{4} \geq a c \\ & \Rightarrow a=1, c=1 \\ & \Rightarrow a=1, c=2 \\ & \Rightarrow a=2, c=1 \\ & b=4 \Rightarrow 16-4 a c \geq 0 \\ & 4 \geq a c\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow a=1, c=1 \\ & \Rightarrow a=1, c=2 \quad \Rightarrow a=2, c=1 \\ & \Rightarrow a=1, c=3 \quad \Rightarrow a=3, c=1 \\ & \Rightarrow a=1, c=4 \quad \Rightarrow a=4, c=1 \\ & \Rightarrow a=2, c=2 \\ & \text { Probability }=\frac{12}{(4)(4)(4)}=\frac{3}{16}=\frac{m}{m} \\ & m+n=19\end{aligned}$

questions.q: Let $\alpha, \beta$ be the distinct roots of the equation $x^2-\left(t^2-5 t+6\right) x+1=0, t \in \mathbb{R}$ and $a_n=\alpha^n+\beta^n$. Then the minimum value of $\frac{a_{2023}+a_{2025}}{a_{2024}}$ is
questions.solution:
by newton's theorem
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{n}+2}-\left(\mathrm{t}^2-5 \mathrm{t}+6\right) \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{n}+1}+\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{n}}=0 \\
& \therefore \mathrm{a}_{2025}+\mathrm{a}_{2023}=\left(\mathrm{t}^2-5 \mathrm{t}+6\right) \mathrm{a}_{2024} \\
& \therefore \frac{\mathrm{a}_{2025}+\mathrm{a}_{2023}}{\mathrm{a}_{2024}}=\mathrm{t}^2-5 \mathrm{t}+6 \\
& \because \mathrm{t}^2-5 \mathrm{t}+6=\left(\mathrm{t}-\frac{5}{2}\right)^2-\frac{1}{4} \\
& \therefore \text { minimum value }=-\frac{1}{4}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.q: Let $x_1, x_2, x_3, x_4$ be the solution of the equation $4 x^4+8 x^3-17 x^2-12 x+9=0$ and $\left(4+x_1^2\right)\left(4+x_2^2\right)\left(4+x_3^2\right)\left(4+x_4^2\right)=\frac{125}{16} m$. Then the value of $m$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 4 x^4+8 x^3-17 x^2-12 x+9 \\ & =4\left(x-x_1\right)\left(x-x_2\right)\left(x-x_3\right)\left(x-x_4\right) \end{aligned}$
Put $x=2 i \&-2 i$ $\begin{aligned} & 64-64 i+68-24 i+9=\left(2 i-x_1\right)\left(2 i-x_2\right)\left(2 i-x_3\right) \\ & \left(2 i-x_4\right) \\ & =141-88 i ....(1)\\ & 64+64 i+68+24 i+9=4\left(-2 i-x_1\right)\left(-2 i-x_2\right)(-2 i \\ & \left.-x_3\right)\left(-2 i-x_4\right) \\ & =141+88 i....(2) \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \frac{125}{16} \mathrm{~m}=\frac{141^2+88^2}{16} \\ & \mathrm{~m}=221 \end{aligned}$

If the set of all possible values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to _______.

And for c+a>b we get,

So, from the equation i, ii & iii we get,

questions.q: Let $A B C$ be an equilateral triangle. A new triangle is formed by joining the middle points of all sides of the triangle $A B C$ and the same process is repeated infinitely many times. If $\mathrm{P}$ is the sum of perimeters and $Q$ is be the sum of areas of all the triangles formed in this process, then :
questions.solution: Area of first $\Delta=\frac{\sqrt{3} \mathrm{a}^2}{4}$ Area of second $\Delta=\frac{\sqrt{3} \mathrm{a}^2}{4} \frac{\mathrm{a}^2}{4}=\frac{\sqrt{3} \mathrm{a}^2}{16}$ Area of third $\Delta=\frac{\sqrt{3} \mathrm{a}^2}{64}$ sum of area $=\frac{\sqrt{3} \mathrm{a}^2}{4}\left(1+\frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{16} \ldots\right)$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{Q}=\frac{\sqrt{3} \mathrm{a}^2}{4} \frac{1}{\frac{3}{4}}=\frac{\mathrm{a}^2}{\sqrt{3}} \\ & \text { perimeter of } 1^{\text {st }} \Delta=3 \mathrm{a} \\ & \text { perimeter of } 2^{\text {nd }} \Delta=\frac{3 \mathrm{a}}{2} \\ & \text { perimeter of } 3^{\text {rd }} \Delta=\frac{3 \mathrm{a}}{4} \\ & \mathrm{P}=3 \mathrm{a}\left(1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}+\ldots\right) \\ & \mathrm{P}=3 \mathrm{a} \cdot 2=6 \mathrm{a} \\ & \mathrm{a}=\frac{\mathrm{P}}{6} \\ & \mathrm{Q}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{P}^2}{36} \\ & \mathrm{P}^2=36 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{Q}\end{aligned}$

questions.q: A software company sets up $m$ number of computer systems to finish an assignment in 17 days. If 4 computer systems crashed on the start of the second day, 4 more computer systems crashed on the start of the third day and so on, then it took 8 more days to finish the assignment. The value of $\mathrm{m}$ is equal to:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 17 \mathrm{~m}=\mathrm{m}+(\mathrm{m}-4)+(\mathrm{m}-4 \times 2) \ldots+\ldots(\mathrm{m}-4 \times 24) \\ & 17 \mathrm{~m}=25 \mathrm{~m}-4(1+2 \ldots 24) \\ & 8 \mathrm{~m}=\frac{4 \cdot 24 \cdot 25}{2}=150\end{aligned}$

\hline \mathrm{X} & 0 & 2 & 4 & 6 & 8 \\
\hline \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{X}) & a & 2 a & a+b & 2 b & 3 b \\
\hline
\end{array}$
is $\frac{46}{9}$, then the variance of the distribution is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \sum P_i=1 \\ & a+2 a+a+b+2 b+3 b=1\end{aligned}$ $4 a+6 b=1$ ...(I) $\mathrm{E}(\mathrm{x})=$ mean $=\frac{46}{9}$ $\begin{aligned} & \sum P_i X_i=\frac{46}{9} \Rightarrow 4 a+4 a+4 b+12 b+24 b=\frac{46}{9} \\ & 8 a+40 b=\frac{46}{9}\end{aligned}$ $4 a+20 b=\frac{23}{9}$ ...(II) Subtract (I) from (II) we get $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{b}=\frac{1}{9} \& \mathrm{a}=\frac{1}{12} \\ & \text { Variance }=\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}{ }^2\right)-\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}\right)^2 \\ & \mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}\right)^2=0^2 \times 9^2+2^2 \times 2 \mathrm{a}+4^2(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})+6^2(2 \mathrm{~b})+8^2(3 \mathrm{~b}) \\ & =24 \mathrm{a}+280 \mathrm{~b} \end{aligned}$
Put $\mathrm{a}=\frac{1}{12} \quad \mathrm{~b}=\frac{1}{9}$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}^2\right)=2+\frac{280}{9}=\frac{298}{9} \\ & \therefore \sigma^2=\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}^2\right)-\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{298}{9}-\left(\frac{46}{9}\right)^2 \\ & \sigma^2=\frac{298}{9}-\frac{2116}{81} \\ & =\frac{566}{81} \end{aligned}$

questions.q: From a lot of 10 items, which include 3 defective items, a sample of 5 items is drawn at random. Let the random variable $\mathrm{X}$ denote the number of defective items in the sample. If the variance of $\mathrm{X}$ is $\sigma^2$, then $96 \sigma^2$ is equal to ______
questions.solution:
$X=$ denotes number of defective
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline \mathrm{x} & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\
\hline \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{x}) & \frac{7}{15} & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{1}{12} \\
\hline \mathrm{x}_1{ }^2 & 0 & 1 & 4 & 9 \\
\hline \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_1{ }^2 & 0 & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{20}{12} & \frac{9}{12} \\
\hline \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}} & 0 & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{10}{12} & \frac{3}{12} \\
\hline
\end{array}$
$\mu=\Sigma \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}=\frac{18}{12}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Sigma \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_1^2=\frac{34}{12} \\
& \sigma^2=\Sigma \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_1^2-(\mu)^2
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{34}{12}-\left(\frac{18}{12}\right)^2=\frac{17}{6}-\frac{9}{4} \\ & \frac{34-27}{12}=\frac{7}{12} \\ & 96 \sigma^2=96 \times \frac{7}{12}=56\end{aligned}$

questions.q: Let $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c} \in \mathrm{N}$ and $\mathrm{a} < \mathrm{b} < \mathrm{c}$. Let the mean, the mean deviation about the mean and the variance of the 5 observations $9,25, \mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c}$ be 18,4 and $\frac{136}{5}$, respectively. Then $2 \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}-\mathrm{c}$ is equal to__________
questions.solution:
$a, b, c \in N \quad a < b < c$
$\overline{\mathrm{x}}=$ mean $=\frac{9+25+\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c}}{5}=18$
$a+b+c=56$
Mean deviation $=\frac{\Sigma\left|\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}-\overline{\mathrm{x}}\right|}{\mathrm{n}}=4$
$\begin{aligned} & =9+7+|18-\mathrm{a}|+|18-\mathrm{b}|+|18-\mathrm{c}|=20 \\ & =|18-\mathrm{a}|+|18-\mathrm{b}|+|18-\mathrm{c}|=4\end{aligned}$
Variance $=\frac{\Sigma\left|\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}-\overline{\mathrm{x}}\right|^2}{\mathrm{n}}=\frac{136}{5}$
$\begin{aligned} & =81+49+|18-a|^2+|18-b|^2+|18-c|^2=136 \\ & =(18-a)^2+(18-b)^2+(18-c)^2=6\end{aligned}$
Possible values $(18-\mathrm{a})^2=1, \quad(18-\mathrm{b})^2=1 \quad(18-\mathrm{c})^2=4$ $\mathrm{a} < \mathrm{b} < \mathrm{c}$
so $\quad \begin{array}{ccc}18-a=1 & 18-b=-1 & 18-c=-2 \\ \mathrm{a}=17 & \mathrm{~b}=19 & \mathrm{c}=20\end{array}$
$a+b+c=56$
$2 \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}-\mathrm{c} 34=19-20=33$

questions.q: The mean and standard deviation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> observations were found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> respectively. On rechecking it was found that an observation was read as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> in place of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>μ</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>σ</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> denote the mean and variance of the correct observations respectively, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi>μ</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>μ</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>σ</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to _________.
questions.solution:
Given: Incorrect mean, x=12 and incorrect standard deviation, σ'=3.

⇒Σxi15=12

⇒Σxi=180

Correct mean is given by,

μ=Σxi-10+1215

⇒μ=18215

Also, σ'=∑xi215-144=3

⇒∑xi215=9+144

⇒∑xi2=2295

Correct Σxi2=2295-100+144=2339

σ2 (correct variance) =233915-182×18215×15

⇒15μ+μ2+σ2=1518215+182×18215×15+233915-182×18215×15

⇒15μ+μ2+σ2=1518215+233915

⇒15μ+μ2+σ2=2521
